questions.question: An application on an EC2 instance needs to read from an S3 bucket. What is the recommended way to grant access?

answers.text: Attach an IAM role to the EC2 instance
Store an IAM user's access keys in a file on the instance

questions.feedback: Use an IAM role so the instance gets automatically rotated temporary credentials, never embed long-lived access keys.

questions.question: Which statement about security groups is correct?

answers.text: They are stateful: allowed inbound traffic has its return traffic automatically permitted
They are stateless and evaluate numbered rules in order

questions.feedback: Security groups are stateful and allow-only. Stateless, numbered, allow+deny rules describe network ACLs.

questions.question: Instances in a private subnet need to download OS updates from the internet but must not be reachable from the internet. What do you use?

answers.text: An internet gateway attached directly to the private subnet

questions.feedback: A NAT gateway allows outbound internet access from private subnets while blocking unsolicited inbound connections.

questions.question: Which approaches help protect data? (Choose all that apply.)

answers.text: Enable KMS encryption at rest on S3, EBS, and RDS
Use TLS (HTTPS) for data in transit
Store database passwords in AWS Secrets Manager with rotation
Commit access keys to your Git repository for convenience

questions.feedback: Encrypt at rest and in transit, and manage secrets in Secrets Manager. Committing keys to source control is a serious security risk.

questions.question: You want EC2 instances to reach Amazon S3 without traffic leaving the AWS network. What do you use?

answers.text: A VPC gateway endpoint for S3
A public IP on each instance

questions.feedback: A VPC endpoint (gateway endpoint for S3/DynamoDB) provides private access to the service without using the public internet.
